mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher short-term corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle phases, or predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum is followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a cumulative return of less than 0.5% over 20 days in >70% of triggered months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic growth that increases aggregate corporate earnings, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.05) with 60-day forward equity returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which suppresses earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Low INDPRO growth is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
